task_completion.description: Verify the original feed data is exported to the correct backup path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the browser downloading a CSV file from the Emoncms feed page, and terminal commands moving, saving, or renaming this file to exactly '/home/ga/attic_temp_backup.csv'.

task_completion.description: Verify the original 10-second 'attic_temp' feed is deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Emoncms Feeds page where the original 'attic_temp' feed (with a 10s interval) is being deleted (e.g., selecting the feed and clicking the trash can icon, or a deletion confirmation).

task_completion.description: Verify a new feed named 'attic_temp' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Emoncms interface (either the Feeds page or the Input process configuration dialog) being used to create a new feed explicitly named 'attic_temp'.

task_completion.description: Verify the new feed is configured with the PHPFina engine and a 600s (10-minute) interval.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Emoncms Feeds page must clearly show the 'attic_temp' feed configured with the 'PHPFina' engine and an interval of '600s' (or '10 mins').

task_completion.description: Verify the 'attic:temp' input is updated to log to the new feed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Emoncms Inputs page must show the input with node 'attic' and name 'temp' having an active process list that includes 'Log to feed' pointing to the newly created 'attic_temp' feed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Emoncms application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must navigate and click through the Emoncms web interface or use valid API commands in the terminal. It should not just type the expected outcomes into a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Verify the backup file is a genuine export and not a faked empty file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation of '/home/ga/attic_temp_backup.csv' should result from a genuine download action or API call (e.g., visible download progress or curl command), not from a command like 'touch' that just creates an empty file.

integrity.description: Verify the browser's developer tools were not used to fake the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) must not be open and used to alter the text of the feed interval, engine, or input process list on the page to fake the migration.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts for the VLM verifier: The backup path must be exactly '/home/ga/attic_temp_backup.csv'. The feed name is 'attic_temp'. The input node is 'attic' and the input name is 'temp'. The target interval for the new feed is 600 seconds (10 minutes), and the engine must be PHPFina. The original interval was 10 seconds.
